SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.safety.depressurization

Class VesselFillingSimulator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.safety.depressurization.VesselFillingSimulator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class VesselFillingSimulator extends Object implements Serializable
Transient filling (charging) simulator for pressurized vessels.

Solves the coupled mass and energy balance for a vessel being charged with gas through an inlet nozzle. The control-volume energy balance for a vessel with inflow only is

dU = h_in * dm_in + Q_wall * dt

where h_in is the specific stagnation enthalpy of the supply gas. Because enthalpy (not internal energy) crosses the boundary, the flow work p*v of the incoming stream is captured automatically, which reproduces the characteristic gas-temperature rise during fast filling (the classic charging problem).

An optional lumped wall model tracks the metal temperature, and the internal gas-to-wall film coefficient may either be a fixed value or computed each step from the Woodfield mixed-convection correlation for charging (see VesselHeatTransferCorrelations). Liner temperature limits (relevant for Type III / Type IV composite hydrogen cylinders) are checked against configurable minimum and maximum temperatures.

References: Woodfield, Monde and Mitsutake (2007), J. Therm. Sci. Tech. 2(2), 180; SAE J2601; ISO 19880-1.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • VesselFillingSimulator

      public VesselFillingSimulator(SystemInterface fluid, double vesselVolume)
      Construct a vessel filling simulator.
      Parameters:
      fluid - initial residual gas in the vessel (same composition as the fill gas); must already have its mixing rule set
      vesselVolume - internal vessel volume in m³; must be positive
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if fluid is null or vesselVolume is not positive

  • Method Details

    • setInletConditions

      public VesselFillingSimulator setInletConditions(double inletTemperatureK, double supplyPressureBara, double massFlowKgPerS)
      Set the supply gas inlet conditions and charging mass flow.
      Parameters:
      inletTemperatureK - supply gas stagnation temperature in K; must be positive
      supplyPressureBara - supply pressure in bara used to evaluate the inlet enthalpy; must be positive
      massFlowKgPerS - charging mass flow into the vessel in kg/s; must be positive
      Returns:
      this simulator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any argument is not positive

    • setTargetPressure

      public VesselFillingSimulator setTargetPressure(double targetBara)
      Set the target final absolute pressure at which to stop charging.
      Parameters:
      targetBara - target absolute pressure in bara; must be positive
      Returns:
      this simulator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if targetBara is not positive

    • setWall

      public VesselFillingSimulator setWall(double wallMass, double wallArea, double wallSpecificHeat, double insideHTC)
      Enable lumped-wall thermal modelling for metal temperature tracking.
      Parameters:
      wallMass - total wall metal mass in kg
      wallArea - internal heat transfer area in m²
      wallSpecificHeat - metal specific heat in J/(kg·K)
      insideHTC - fixed inside film heat transfer coefficient in W/(m²·K) used unless the Woodfield correlation is enabled
      Returns:
      this simulator for chaining

    • setWoodfieldHeatTransfer

      public VesselFillingSimulator setWoodfieldHeatTransfer(double inletNozzleDiameterM, double vesselHeightM)
      Enable the Woodfield mixed-convection correlation for the internal film coefficient during filling. The coefficient is recomputed each timestep from the inlet-jet Reynolds number and the vessel-height Rayleigh number.
      Parameters:
      inletNozzleDiameterM - inlet nozzle diameter in m; must be positive
      vesselHeightM - internal vessel height in m; must be positive
      Returns:
      this simulator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either dimension is not positive

    • setLinerTemperatureLimits

      public VesselFillingSimulator setLinerTemperatureLimits(double minTemperatureK, double maxTemperatureK)
      Set the liner (or metal) temperature limits used for acceptance checking during filling. For a Type IV composite hydrogen cylinder the liner limits are typically −40 °C to +85 °C.
      Parameters:
      minTemperatureK - minimum allowable gas temperature in K
      maxTemperatureK - maximum allowable gas temperature in K
      Returns:
      this simulator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxTemperatureK is not greater than minTemperatureK

    • setTimeStep

      public VesselFillingSimulator setTimeStep(double dt)
      Set the integration timestep (default 1 s).
      Parameters:
      dt - timestep in s; must be positive
      Returns:
      this simulator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dt is not positive

    • setMaxTime

      public VesselFillingSimulator setMaxTime(double tMax)
      Set the maximum simulation time (default 600 s).
      Parameters:
      tMax - maximum time in s; must be positive
      Returns:
      this simulator for chaining
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if tMax is not positive

    • run

      public VesselFillingSimulator.VesselFillingResult run()
      Run the transient filling simulation and return the time-series result.
      Returns:
      result containing time, pressure, temperature, mass and metal-temperature trajectories
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the inlet conditions have not been configured

    • computeInletSpecificEnthalpy

      private double computeInletSpecificEnthalpy()
      Computes the specific stagnation enthalpy of the supply gas at the configured inlet conditions.
      Returns:
      inlet specific enthalpy in J/kg

    • currentInsideHtc

      private double currentInsideHtc(double density, double mass, double tempK)
      Determines the internal gas-to-wall film coefficient for the current step, using either the Woodfield correlation or the fixed configured value.
      Parameters:
      density - current gas density in kg/m³
      mass - current gas mass in kg
      tempK - current gas temperature in K
      Returns:
      inside film heat-transfer coefficient in W/(m²·K)

    • scaleMoles

      private void scaleMoles(double factor)
      Scale the total mole inventory of the fluid by the given factor while preserving composition. See DepressurizationSimulator#scaleMoles(double) for the rationale.
      Parameters:
      factor - multiplicative scaling factor; values ≤ 0, NaN or equal to 1 are ignored
